author: barnabasbusa
content: welcome to the bleeding edge lads

author: omerfirmak
content: <@774033563732541451> , do you think you can extend your r2 bucket work to expose a WS stream of `statelessInput` bytes for new blocks on devnets? So we can execute devnet block in real time.

author: jsign
content:
Maybe -- although my original intention is that the R2 bucket is more like a batch-pull style archive of "batches" not blocks.

But the rockstar at the infra level should be `zkboost` for RTP and the only way to strengthen that is using it. This tool I did was for having this R2 bucket idea sooner without putting much pressure on touching `zkboost` yet.
My idea is that this "upload blocks input (or batches of them) to an R2 bucket" is some feature we can add to zkboost, so we keep things simple at infra level. 

I worked around it now with doing it in `zkevm-benchmark-workload` simply because we are simplifying some relationships between `ere-guests`, `zkboost`, `zkevm-benchmark-workload` -- but ideally we should aim or as least and simple infra as possible. I already chatted about this with <@466102668138512394> , just that we're still in puting some things in the right place to minimize long term repo and infra complexity and duplication.

So a very long answer to say: "maybe" but prob this archiving feature might be in zkboost, which can use soon for devnets and think from there.
